reviews.review_msg: 479's exercises involved us pretty much learning a completely new API for every single one.  You can tell he really cares about his students and their quality of education. Don't take if you're not motivated or can't attend lectures as his slides are always explained in detail in class, so you wont learn much from looking at them

Amazing prof, a fount of knowledge. He is very passionate about software engineering and is always willing to answer questions on it and direct you to relevant research. He does expect more from students than other profs so be wary if you aren't prepared to spend the time on the class. It has been difficult for me so far but definetly worth it.
Makes exam questions have answers based on a technicality. Makes assignments have acoustic clues that you need to decipher to figure out what he really wants.

Tao is very passionate and it's clear he's an expert, but he can come off as arrogant and looks down on students who are struggling. He also speaks fast and bases exams off of things he says, so it's difficult to keep up when you can't hear what he saying but it's important. He also seemed to get a kick off of people being upset over the midterm.
